QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_123

question.text: Following the SEBI circular of May 2025, which of the following correctly states the expiry days for equity derivatives on Indian exchanges?

answer.text: NSE equity derivatives expire on the last Tuesday of the expiry month, while BSE equity derivatives expire on the last Thursday of the expiry month
Both NSE and BSE equity derivatives expire on the last Thursday of the expiry month
NSE expires on the last Friday and BSE expires on the last Wednesday of the expiry month
NSE expires on the last Thursday and BSE expires on the last Tuesday of the expiry month

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Per the SEBI circular issued in May 2025: - NSE equity derivatives: Last TUESDAY of the expiry month - BSE equity derivatives: Last THURSDAY of the expiry month
If the scheduled expiry day is a trading holiday, the contract expires on the immediately preceding trading day.
**The Trap:** Previously, NSE also expired on Thursday. The change to Tuesday for NSE is a recent and important regulatory update. The BSE expiry remains on Thursday. Don't reverse the two.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This is a must-know regulatory fact for the Series VIII exam. Expect at least one question on expiry days.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NSE = Tuesday expiry. BSE = Thursday expiry (per SEBI May 2025 circular).
